single: {token_render_style_instruction} Exactly one option loop is equivalent to the reference loop. Rotations count as equivalent; reversed or reflected order does not.
{token_render_style_instruction} Select the only option that preserves the same cyclic order as the reference. Rotations are allowed, but reversal is not.
{token_render_style_instruction} Which labeled option matches the reference up to rotation, without reversing the order?
{token_render_style_instruction} Identify the single valid option loop. A valid loop may be rotated but not reflected.
{token_render_style_instruction} What label marks the equivalent option loop, treating rotated loops as the same and reflected loops as different?
swap_repair: {token_render_style_instruction} The broken loop has numbered token positions. Which swap option repairs it so its cyclic order matches the reference up to rotation?
{token_render_style_instruction} Choose the swap of two numbered positions that fixes the broken loop. The repaired loop may be rotated relative to the reference, but not reversed.
{token_render_style_instruction} Which labeled position swap would make the broken loop preserve the same cyclic order as the reference?
{token_render_style_instruction} Select the only swap option that repairs the broken loop into a rotated copy of the reference order.
{token_render_style_instruction} Swap the two numbered positions named by one option. Which option makes the broken loop match the reference cyclic order?
insertion_position: {token_render_style_instruction} One reference token is missing from the partial loop. Which labeled gap should contain the missing token so the cyclic order matches up to rotation?
{token_render_style_instruction} Choose the labeled gap where the missing reference token belongs. The completed loop may be rotated relative to the reference, but not reversed.
{token_render_style_instruction} Which gap label places the missing token correctly in the partial loop?
{token_render_style_instruction} Select the gap that completes the partial loop as a rotated copy of the reference.
{token_render_style_instruction} One token from the reference is absent from the partial loop. Which labeled gap restores the reference cyclic order?

task:insertion_position_label_query.annotation_hint: Set annotation to the image-pixel bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1] around the selected labeled gap badge.
